cases.prompt: how professional does my repo look?
review my repo setup before I make it public
score my repo against OSS best practices
is my project ready for outside contributors?
run the linter on the codebase

expected_behavior: collects evidence mechanically first: gh api community profile (health_percentage), gh repo view metadata, file presence, protections/security settings
scores each of the six categories (README, discoverability, community files, intake templates, protections, vital signs) and assigns an overall grade
returns a top-5 prioritized fix list where each fix names the focused skill that owns it
applies no fixes unless the user asks

cases.prompt: review my repo before I flip it public
expected_behavior: adds public-flip-specific checks: PVR enablement, secret-scanning of history, license presence, no personal/private files about to become visible
produces an ordered pre-flight list distinguishing blockers from nice-to-haves
recommends enabling protections that only work on public repos right after the flip

cases.prompt: audit this - it's just my solo hobby project
expected_behavior: right-sizes the rubric: marks governance/templates as intentionally deferred rather than failing, per the checklist's stage guidance
still flags the non-negotiables (license, readme basics, no leaked secrets)
keeps the report short and actionable for a solo maintainer
